title: The Global Challenge: Balancing the Risks and Promises of AI
subtitle: From government warnings to corporate advertising, the world is struggling to define the role of artificial intelligence.

summary.en: Artificial intelligence is becoming a major focus for global leaders and technology companies alike. Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper has warned that international rules are necessary to prevent a disaster similar to the invention of nuclear weapons. Meanwhile, tech companies are promoting AI through controversial advertisements that imagine historical figures using modern tools. Experts remain divided on whether these systems are truly helpful for human collaboration. The debate highlights the tension between the potential benefits of new technology and the serious risks it poses to security. Governments are now looking for ways to manage these powerful tools before they fall into the wrong hands.
summary.zh: 人工智慧正成為全球領袖與科技公司共同關注的焦點。外交大臣庫柏警告，必須制定國際規範以防止類似核子武器發明所帶來的災難。與此同時，科技公司正透過具爭議性的廣告來推廣人工智慧，在廣告中想像歷史人物使用現代工具。專家們對於這些系統是否真的有助於人類協作仍存有分歧。這場辯論凸顯了新科技的潛在益處與其對安全構成的嚴重風險之間的緊張關係。各國政府目前正尋求在這些強大工具落入錯誤的人手中之前，設法加以控管。

paragraphs.en: Artificial intelligence (AI) has moved to the center of global politics and public debate. As these computer systems become more powerful, leaders are warning that the world must act quickly to create international rules. According to The Guardian, Yvette Cooper, the Foreign Secretary, recently stated that AI could pose a threat to humanity similar to the power of nuclear weapons. She argued that just as the world had to create agreements after seeing the destruction caused by nuclear technology, governments must now cooperate to control how AI is developed.
paragraphs.zh: 人工智慧（AI）已成為全球政治與公共辯論的核心議題。隨著這些電腦系統變得日益強大，各國領袖紛紛警告，世界必須迅速採取行動，制定國際規範。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，外交大臣庫柏（Yvette Cooper）近期指出，人工智慧對人類造成的威脅，可能與核子武器的威力相當。她主張，正如世界在目睹核子技術帶來的毀滅後必須達成協議一樣，各國政府現在也必須合作，共同控管人工智慧的發展方式。

paragraphs.en: Cooper believes that AI will be the most important foreign policy issue for the next two years. In a report for the Chatham House thinktank, she explained that while AI has amazing potential, it also carries huge risks. She is particularly worried about "malign actors"—people or groups with bad intentions—who might use this technology to cause harm. These threats could come from criminal groups, extremists, or other organizations that want to weaken democracy. Because the United States may no longer play the same role in maintaining global peace as it once did, Cooper suggests that other countries must work together to build a new security system.
paragraphs.zh: 庫柏認為，人工智慧將是未來兩年最重要的外交政策議題。她在為查塔姆研究所（Chatham House）撰寫的一份報告中解釋，雖然人工智慧具備驚人的潛力，但也伴隨著巨大的風險。她特別擔心「惡意行為者」——即那些心懷不軌的個人或團體——可能會利用這項技術來造成傷害。這些威脅可能來自犯罪集團、極端分子或其他意圖削弱民主的組織。由於美國可能不再像過去那樣在維護全球和平中扮演相同角色，庫柏建議其他國家必須攜手合作，建立一套新的安全體系。

paragraphs.en: While politicians focus on safety and security, technology companies are working to make AI a part of everyday life. However, these efforts are not always well-received by the public. The Verge recently reported on a new commercial from Google that shows the "founding fathers" of the United States using AI tools to write the Declaration of Independence. In the video, historical figures use digital documents and AI assistants to organize their work. The commercial suggests that these tools could have helped them collaborate more efficiently.
paragraphs.zh: 當政治人物聚焦於安全與防護時，科技公司正致力於將人工智慧融入日常生活。然而，這些努力並不總是受到大眾歡迎。《邊緣》（The Verge）近期報導了一則 Google 的新廣告，內容展示美國的「開國元勳」使用人工智慧工具來撰寫《獨立宣言》。在影片中，這些歷史人物使用數位文件與人工智慧助理來整理工作。該廣告暗示，這些工具本可以幫助他們更有效率地進行協作。

paragraphs.en: This advertisement has faced strong criticism. Many people found the idea of using modern AI to write such an important historical document to be strange and disrespectful. Critics argue that the commercial fails to show how AI is actually useful for serious political or human work. Angus Johnston, a history professor at CUNY, noted that even in a lighthearted joke, it is difficult to prove that AI is a helpful tool for human organization. The reaction to the commercial shows that many people are uncomfortable with how companies are trying to connect new technology to serious human history.
paragraphs.zh: 這則廣告面臨強烈批評。許多人認為，利用現代人工智慧來撰寫如此重要的歷史文件，這種構想既奇怪又不尊重歷史。批評者指出，該廣告未能展現人工智慧在嚴肅的政治或人類工作上究竟有何實質助益。紐約市立大學（CUNY）的歷史學教授強斯頓（Angus Johnston）指出，即便是在輕鬆的玩笑中，也很難證明人工智慧是人類組織工作的實用工具。大眾對這則廣告的反應顯示，許多人對於企業試圖將新科技與嚴肅的人類歷史連結起來的做法感到不適。

paragraphs.en: These two stories highlight a growing divide in how society views AI. On one hand, governments see it as a powerful force that needs strict guardrails—rules or limits designed to prevent accidents or misuse. They are worried about state-backed groups and terrorists using AI to attack western societies. On the other hand, companies are trying to normalize AI by putting it into our daily tasks, from writing documents to managing schedules. This creates a confusing situation for the public, who must decide if AI is a helpful assistant or a dangerous risk.
paragraphs.zh: 這兩則故事凸顯了社會對人工智慧看法日益分歧的現象。一方面，政府將其視為一股強大的力量，需要嚴格的防護欄——即旨在防止意外或濫用的規則或限制。他們擔心國家支持的團體與恐怖分子會利用人工智慧攻擊西方社會。另一方面，企業正試圖透過將人工智慧應用於撰寫文件、管理行程等日常任務，使其常態化。這為大眾帶來了困惑，人們必須自行判斷人工智慧究竟是實用的助手，還是危險的隱憂。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the challenge for world leaders will be to find a balance. Cooper’s warning suggests that waiting for a disaster is not an option. She believes that the world cannot afford to wait until something goes wrong before creating international agreements. This means that countries will need to negotiate new ways to share information and set safety standards. At the same time, the public will continue to watch how companies market these tools. If technology firms continue to use controversial or "corny" advertising, it may make it even harder for people to trust the AI systems they are being asked to use.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，世界領袖面臨的挑戰將是如何取得平衡。庫柏的警告暗示，坐等災難發生並非選項。她認為，世界無法承擔等到出事後才制定國際協議的後果。這意味著各國需要協商出新的方式來共享資訊並設定安全標準。與此同時，大眾將持續關注企業如何行銷這些工具。如果科技公司持續使用具爭議性或「老套」的廣告，可能會讓民眾更難以信任他們被要求使用的人工智慧系統。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, the future of AI will depend on whether governments can work together to manage the risks while allowing the technology to grow. As Cooper noted, the world is at a dangerous moment. Whether it is through new security agreements or better public understanding, the goal is to ensure that AI serves the interests of humanity rather than threatening it. The next two years will likely be a critical time for deciding how these powerful machines fit into our world.
paragraphs.zh: 歸根究柢，人工智慧的未來將取決於各國政府能否在允許技術成長的同時，共同合作以控管風險。正如庫柏所言，世界正處於危險的時刻。無論是透過新的安全協議還是提升公眾認知，目標都是確保人工智慧服務於人類利益，而非威脅人類。未來兩年對於決定這些強大的機器如何融入我們的世界，將是關鍵時期。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be the most important part or focus of something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（中心），這裡作動詞用。文中：Artificial intelligence (AI) has moved to the center of global politics and public debate.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To involve or have a specific quality or consequence.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 帶有、具有（某種特質或後果）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（搬運），這裡指抽象的「具有風險」。文中：In a report for the Chatham House thinktank, she explained that while AI has amazing potential, it also carries huge risks.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To make something seem normal or acceptable.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 使……正常化、使……變得習以為常。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此詞在科技語境中常指讓大眾接受新技術。文中：On the other hand, companies are trying to normalize AI by putting it into our daily tasks, from writing documents to managing schedules.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be suitable or appropriate for a particular place or situation.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作形容詞（健康的/合身的），這裡作動詞指融入社會。文中：The next two years will likely be a critical time for deciding how these powerful machines fit into our world.

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: AI poses ‘Hiroshima’-style threat to humanity without global rules, says Cooper

sources.name: The Verge
sources.title: Infuriating Google commercial imagines the founding fathers embracing AI

quiz.question: According to Yvette Cooper, why is it necessary for governments to cooperate on AI regulation now?

options.A: Because AI companies are currently failing to make a profit.
options.B: Because the United States is potentially changing its role in global security.
options.C: Because criminal groups have already successfully launched a major cyberattack.
options.D: Because AI technology has already caused destruction similar to nuclear weapons.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第二段提到，由於美國可能不再像過去那樣維持全球和平，Cooper 建議各國必須合作建立新的安全系統。選項 A 文中未提及；選項 C 文中僅提到「擔心」惡意行為者，並未指出已經發生大規模攻擊；選項 D 錯誤，文中是將 AI 的「潛在威脅」與核武相比，而非已經發生了毀滅性災難。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the public's reaction to Google's commercial?

options.A: The public is excited to see AI used in historical contexts.
options.B: People are worried that the commercial will make AI too expensive.
options.C: The public feels that AI should not be trivialized by connecting it to serious historical events.
options.D: Most people believe the commercial accurately depicts how the Declaration of Independence was written.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段，民眾對廣告感到不適，因為將現代 AI 與重要的歷史文獻連結顯得「奇怪且不尊重」，這顯示大眾認為 AI 不應被用在嚴肅的歷史議題上。選項 A 與文意相反；選項 B 文中未提及價格問題；選項 D 不正確，因為該廣告顯然是虛構的創意，而非歷史事實。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the term 'guardrails' mean in the context of AI regulation?

options.A: Physical barriers built around computer servers.
options.B: Strict rules or limits designed to prevent misuse.
options.C: Software programs that block internet access.
options.D: A team of security guards hired to protect data.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文中第五段提到政府將 AI 視為需要「strict guardrails」的力量，並緊接著解釋為「rules or limits designed to prevent accidents or misuse」，意指為了防止 AI 被濫用或發生意外而設立的規範與限制。選項 A、C、D 皆為字面上的誤解，忽略了文中明確提供的定義。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the future of AI?

options.A: AI will inevitably lead to a global disaster that cannot be avoided.
options.B: Technology companies are the only ones capable of deciding the future of AI.
options.C: The public is entirely against the use of AI in any form of daily life.
options.D: Society faces a critical challenge in balancing AI's potential benefits with necessary global safety regulations.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。文章整體探討了政府對安全監管的重視與科技公司對 AI 生活化的推廣之間的拉鋸，並強調未來兩年各國需在風險控管與技術發展間找到平衡。選項 A 過於悲觀；選項 B 忽略了政府的角色；選項 C 錯誤，文中僅提到大眾對特定廣告感到不適，並非全面反對 AI。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
